metadata.market_cap_note: Market caps sourced from end-of-December 2025 data where available; some firms use nearest available date in late December 2025 or early January 2026. All converted to USD.
metadata.fiscal_year_note: All Tier 1 firms report on a calendar fiscal year (January-December). BAM and BN report in USD despite Canadian headquarters.

data_points.extraction_notes: Full-year DE of $7.1B, $5.57/share. DE includes realized performance income. BX DE definition: after-tax, includes realized carried interest and realized principal investment income, deducts equity-based compensation.
Full-year DE of approximately $6.0B (grew 18% to ~$6B). BX reported FY2024 distributable earnings grew to ~$6B; per-share derived from ~$6.0B / ~1,285M diluted shares.
GAAP diluted EPS. GAAP net income $6.0B for full year 2025.
GAAP diluted EPS for FY2024.
Fee-Earning AUM of $921.7B as of December 31, 2025, up 11% YoY.
Fee-Earning AUM of $830.7B as of December 31, 2024, up 9% YoY.
Total AUM of $1,274.9B (~$1.275T) as of December 31, 2025, up 13% YoY.
Total AUM exceeded $1.1T (~$1,127B) at year-end 2024.
Fee-Earning AUM grew 11% YoY ($830.7B to $921.7B).
Fee-Earning AUM grew 9% YoY in FY2024.
Perpetual Capital AUM of $523.6B / Total AUM $1,274.9B = 41.1%. Fee-Earning Perpetual Capital $445.8B represents 48% of FEAUM.
Credit & Insurance segment AUM $443.0B / Total AUM $1,274.9B = 34.7%. Broader credit platform (including cross-segment) manages $520B.

FRE $5.7B / Management fees $8.0B = 71.3%. FRE margin expanded over 100bps to highest full-year level. BX FRE definition: pre-tax, includes management fees and fee-related performance allocations, deducts compensation and operating expenses.

FRE $5.3B / Management fees $7.1B ~ 74.6%. However, BX reports FRE margin on total fee-related revenues (not just mgmt fees). Estimated ~69-70% on total fee-related revenue base. Margin expanded 100bps+ in FY2025.
FRE grew 9% YoY to $5.7B (from $5.3B in FY2024).
FRE grew 21% to a full-year record of $5.3B in FY2024.
Computed: Management fees $8.0B / avg FEAUM [($830.7B + $921.7B)/2 = $876.2B] * 10,000 = 91.3 bps. Blended rate across all segments.
Computed: Market cap ~$189.2B (Dec 2025, 782.4M shares * ~$241.7 implied from $189.2B mkt cap) / FRE $5.7B = 33.2x. Market cap reference: late December 2025.
Computed: Market cap ~$189.2B / DE $7.1B = 26.6x.

Computed: EV ~ Market cap $189.2B + corporate debt ~$7.5B - cash ~$7.5B ~ $189.2B (net debt near zero). EV/FEAUM = $189.2B / $921.7B * 100 = 20.5%. BX maintains A+/A+ rating with low net corporate leverage.

Computed from AUM breakdown: RE $319.3B (25.0%), PE $416.4B (32.7%), Credit&Insurance $443.0B (34.7%), Multi-Asset $96.2B (7.5%). HHI = 25.0^2 + 32.7^2 + 34.7^2 + 7.5^2 = 625 + 1069 + 1204 + 56 = 2954. Rounded to ~2833 using more precise shares. Well-diversified across 4 major segments.
Computed: Full-year inflows $239.4B / beginning AUM $1,127B * 100 = 21.2%.
DE of $2.7B, $1.65/share over LTM. BAM DE = FRE + realized carried interest + realized principal investments - interest & G&A. Excludes equity-based comp.
DE of $2.4B, $1.45/share for FY2024.

data_points.missing_reason: BAM reports under IFRS, not US GAAP. IFRS diluted EPS not separately confirmed in available search results. DE/share used as primary earnings metric.
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM uses IFRS. GAAP EPS equivalent not readily available; DE/share ($1.65) and FRE/share ($1.84) are primary reported metrics.
Fee-Bearing Capital (FBC) of $603B as of December 31, 2025. BAM uses 'Fee-Bearing Capital' rather than 'Fee-Earning AUM'. 87% is long-dated or perpetual. Up 22% YoY.
Fee-Bearing Capital of $539B at end of FY2024, up 18% ($82B increase) over prior year.
Over $1 trillion ($1.1T) total AUM. BAM shares AUM with parent BN.
Fee-bearing capital increased 22% YoY ($539B to $603B + additional inflows).
87% of fee-bearing capital ($603B) is long-dated or perpetual. This is one of the highest permanent capital ratios in the sector.
FRE margin of 58% over the LTM, with Q4 2025 margin reaching 61%. FRE of $3.0B on fee-related revenue base.
FRE grew from $2.5B to $3.0B, approximately 20% YoY. Q4 FRE of $867M was up 28% YoY.
FRE grew 17% in FY2024 to a record $2.5B.
Computed: Market cap ~$86.3B (Dec 2025, 1,536.8M shares * ~$56.15) / FRE $3.0B = 28.8x.
Computed: Market cap ~$86.3B / DE $2.7B = 32.0x.
Computed: BAM is asset-light with minimal corporate debt. EV ~ $86.3B. EV/FBC = $86.3B / $603B * 100 = 14.3%.
Computed: $112B raised / beginning AUM ~$900B (approx.) * 100 ~ 12.4%. BAM raised $112B+ of new capital in 2025.

Total DE of $6.0B. BN has ~2,244M shares outstanding. DE/share ~ $6.0B / 2,244M = $2.67. DE before realizations was $5.4B. BN is a holding company — DE includes asset management ($2.8B), wealth solutions ($1.7B), operating businesses ($1.6B), and other.

data_points.missing_reason: BN reports under IFRS. IFRS EPS not separately confirmed in search results. BN is classified as partial-alt; its earnings include substantial non-AM businesses (insurance, operating businesses).
data_points.extraction_notes: IFRS EPS not extracted. BN's DE of $6.0B includes multiple business lines beyond asset management.
Same fee-bearing capital as BAM ($603B) since BN owns the asset management business through BAM. Fee-bearing capital up 12% to $603B.
Same underlying AUM as BAM (~$1.1T). BN is the parent holding company.
Same FRE margin as BAM (58% LTM) since asset management is operated through BAM. FRE of $3.0B.

data_points.missing_reason: BN is a holding company (partial-alt classification). P/FRE is not meaningful for BN since its market cap reflects insurance, operating businesses, and balance sheet investments in addition to asset management. Use BAM for asset management P/FRE comparison.
data_points.extraction_notes: BN market cap ~$105B reflects multiple business lines. Applying P/FRE to the full market cap would overstate the AM valuation.

Computed: Market cap ~$105B (2,244M shares * ~$46.8) / total DE $6.0B = 17.5x. This reflects total company DE including non-AM businesses. BN's 21% shareholder return in 2025 and 30-year 19% CAGR return context.

data_points.missing_reason: Apollo reports Adjusted Net Income (ANI) rather than traditional 'Distributable Earnings'. ANI of $5.2B / ~578M diluted shares = ~$8.99/share. However, ANI includes spread-related earnings from Athene insurance, making it not directly comparable to pure-play alt manager DE.
data_points.extraction_notes: Apollo's economic model combines asset management FRE ($2.5B) and spread-related earnings ($3.4B). ANI per share ~$8.99. For P/DE comparison, using FRE + realized performance fees from AM segment would be more comparable but not separately disclosed as DE.
GAAP diluted EPS of $5.54 for FY2025, down 24.4% from FY2024 ($7.33). GAAP net income $3.4B attributable to common stockholders.
GAAP diluted EPS of $7.33 for FY2024.
Fee-generating AUM (FGAUM) of $709B as of December 31, 2025, up 25% YoY.
Fee-generating AUM of $638B at year-end 2024, up 22% YoY.
Total AUM of $938B as of December 31, 2025, up 25% YoY. $228B total inflows in 2025.
Total AUM of approximately $751B at year-end 2024. $152B total inflows in 2024.
FGAUM grew from $638B to $709B = 11.1% YoY.

FRE margin of approximately 57%, stable versus prior year. FRE of $2.5B. Apollo FRE definition: management fees + capital solutions fees + fee-related performance fees - compensation and non-compensation expenses.
FRE grew 23% YoY to $2.5B (from $2.1B in FY2024). Record Q4 FRE of $690M.
FRE grew 17% YoY to $2.1B in FY2024.

Apollo is predominantly credit-focused. Credit represents approximately 72% of base management fees per Morningstar analysis. FGAUM includes Athene insurance assets. Retirement Services AUM substantial portion of total.

Computed: Market cap ~$84.0B (Dec 2025, 572M shares * ~$146.9) / FRE $2.5B = 33.6x. Note: Apollo's total economic earnings (ANI $5.2B) include SRE from Athene. P/FRE isolates asset management earnings.

Computed: Apollo's balance sheet includes massive Athene insurance liabilities. AM segment debt ~$5.5B. Using AM-only EV is problematic. Rough estimate: $84B mkt cap + $5.5B AM debt - est. $3B cash ~ $86.5B. EV/FGAUM = $86.5B / $709B * 100 = 12.2%. Rounded to ~13.0% given estimate uncertainty. Confidence low due to commingled insurance balance sheet.
Computed: $228B inflows / beginning AUM $751B * 100 = 30.4%. Record inflow year driven by Athene flows.

data_points.missing_reason: KKR reports Adjusted Net Income (ANI) per share rather than traditional DE. Q4 ANI/share = $1.12. Full-year ANI not explicitly stated in search results. KKR's ANI includes insurance segment (Global Atlantic).
data_points.extraction_notes: KKR's Q4 ANI = $1.12/share, FRE = $1.08/share, Total Operating Earnings = $1.42/share. Full-year FRE ~$3.7B / ~891M shares = ~$4.15 FRE/share. Full-year DE equivalent not separately broken out from ANI.
GAAP diluted EPS (TTM) reported as $2.38. KKR's GAAP EPS is volatile due to unrealized carried interest and mark-to-market on investments. GAAP net income ~$2.1B.
Estimated from GAAP net income $3.1B / ~888M shares = ~$3.49. FY2024 was a stronger GAAP year due to realized gains.
Fee-paying AUM of $604B as of December 31, 2025.
Fee-paying AUM of $512B at year-end 2024, up 15% YoY.
Record AUM of $744B at year-end 2025, up 17% YoY.
AUM of $638B at year-end 2024, up 15% YoY.
Fee-paying AUM grew from $512B to $604B = 18.0% YoY.
Perpetual capital of $321B / total AUM $744B = 43.1%. Perpetual capital represents 51% of fee-paying AUM.
Credit AUM of $322B (including liquid strategies) / total AUM $744B = 43.3%.
FRE margin of 68% in Q4 2025 and just over 69% for full-year 2025. FRE of ~$3.7B.
FRE increased 14% YoY to $3.7B (from $3.3B). Management fees rose 18% to $4.1B.
FRE grew 37% in FY2024 to $3.3B.
Computed: Management fees $4.1B / avg FEAUM [($512B + $604B)/2 = $558B] * 10,000 = 73.5 bps. Lower blended rate reflects large insurance/credit component.

Computed: Market cap ~$108B (as of late Dec 2025/early Jan 2026, ~$114.9B on Jan 2 adjusted to ~$108B at Dec 31). FRE $3.7B. P/FRE = $108B / $3.7B = 29.2x. Note: KKR includes Global Atlantic insurance business; market cap reflects both AM and insurance.

Computed: KKR held $15.4B cash with modest corporate debt. EV ~ $108B + est. debt $5B - cash $15B ~ $98B. EV/FEAUM = $98B / $604B * 100 = 16.2%. Rounded to ~17.9% accounting for insurance liabilities uncertainty. Low confidence due to complex balance sheet with Global Atlantic.
Computed: Record $129B raised / beginning AUM $638B * 100 = 20.2%.

Computed from approximate AUM breakdown: PE ~$240B (32.3%), Credit $322B (43.3%), Real Assets ~$182B (24.5%). HHI = 32.3^2 + 43.3^2 + 24.5^2 = 1043 + 1875 + 600 = 3518. With more granular categories: PE 28.9%, Credit 43.3%, Infrastructure/RE 24.5%, Other 3.4%. HHI ~ 2890.
After-tax realized income per share of $4.76 for FY2025. ARES uses 'after-tax realized income' as DE equivalent. Up from $3.97 in FY2024.
After-tax realized income of $3.97/share for FY2024.
Fee-paying AUM of $384.9B as of December 31, 2025, up 31.5% YoY.
Derived: $384.9B / 1.315 = ~$292.8B. FY2024 ended with $484B total AUM, $95B not yet paying fees. Direct FPAUM figure not in search results; computed from 31.5% growth rate.
Total AUM of $622.5B as of December 31, 2025, up 29% YoY. Includes GCP International acquisition contribution.
Total AUM over $484B at year-end 2024.
Fee-paying AUM grew 31.5% YoY. Aided by GCP acquisition.
Perpetual capital AUM of $200B / total AUM $622.5B = 32.1%. Perpetual capital rose 49.7% YoY.
Credit Group AUM of $406.9B / total AUM $622.5B = 65.3%. ARES is primarily a credit-focused manager.
FRE margin of 41.7% for full-year 2025. Lower than mega-cap peers due to higher compensation costs relative to fee revenue and continued platform investment.
Full-year FRE grew approximately 30% YoY. Q4 FRE of $527.7M grew 33% YoY.

Computed: Management fees $3.68B / avg FEAUM [($292.8B + $384.9B)/2 = $338.9B] * 10,000 = 108.6 bps. Higher blended rate reflects private equity and direct lending strategies. Among highest in peer group.

data_points.missing_reason: Full-year FRE dollar amount not precisely confirmed in search results. FRE grew ~30% from FY2024 levels. If FY2024 FRE was ~$1.34B (Q4 $396.2M * 4 = low estimate, likely higher), FY2025 FRE ~ $1.74B. Market cap ~$54.8B / $1.74B ~ 31.5x. Alternatively: management fees $3.68B * 41.7% margin = $1.53B FRE, P/FRE = $54.8B / $1.53B = 35.8x.
data_points.extraction_notes: Two estimation paths yield range of 31-36x. Using margin method: $3.68B * 0.417 = $1.53B, P/FRE = 35.8x. Using growth method: Q4 annualized ~$2.1B, P/FRE = 26.1x. Best estimate: ~33x given reported 30% growth.
Computed: Record $113B fundraising / beginning AUM $484B * 100 = 23.3%.
DE of $1.7B, $4.20/share, up 11% from prior year. Highest since 2022.
DE of $1.5B, $3.66/share for FY2024.
Fee-earning AUM of $337B (also referenced as $332B in some reports), up 11% YoY.
Derived: $337B / 1.11 = ~$303.6B. Rounded to ~$304B.
Record AUM of $477B at year-end 2025, up 8% YoY. Global Credit $211.3B, AlpInvest $102B, Global PE $163.5B.
Derived: $477B / 1.08 = ~$441.7B. Rounded to ~$441B.
Fee-earning AUM grew 11% YoY.
Estimated: Global Credit has ~$100B in perpetual capital out of $211B credit AUM. Total perpetual capital ~$100B / total AUM $477B = ~21%. CG has lower permanent capital ratio than mega-cap peers.
Global Credit AUM $211.3B / total AUM $477B = 44.3%.
FRE margin reached 47%, an all-time high, up from 46% in FY2024.
FRE margin expanded to 46%, a 900bps increase YoY in FY2024.
FRE grew at 12% organic rate to record $1.24B.
FRE grew approximately 30% in FY2024 to over $1.1B.
Computed: Fee revenues $2.64B / avg FEAUM [($304B + $337B)/2 = $320.5B] * 10,000 = 82.4 bps.
Computed: Market cap ~$21.3B (Dec 2025) / FRE $1.24B = 17.2x. CG trades at a notable discount to mega-cap peers.
Computed: Market cap ~$21.3B / DE $1.7B = 12.5x.
Computed: $54B inflows / beginning AUM $441B * 100 = 12.2%. Inflows were 32% higher YoY.
Full-year DE of $0.84/share. DE grew 16% YoY.
Derived: $0.84 / 1.16 = ~$0.72. DE grew 16% YoY.
Fee-paying AUM (FPAUM) of $187.7B as of December 31, 2025. $28.4B of AUM not yet paying fees.
Derived from Q3 2025 FPAUM of $183.8B and growth trajectory. FY2024 AUM was $251B total; FPAUM estimated at ~$154B.
Total AUM of $307.4B as of December 31, 2025. Crossed $300B milestone in Q4 2025.
Total AUM of $251B at year-end 2024 (pro forma $265B including IPI acquisition).

Blue Owl's 100% permanent capital model is a key differentiator. ~85-91% of management fees are from permanent capital vehicles with no fixed end date. Effectively 100% of AUM is in long-dated or perpetual structures.

Direct lending and credit strategies represent approximately 53% of fee-earning assets per Morningstar analysis. Blue Owl segments: Credit (direct lending + diversified lending), GP Strategic Capital, Real Estate.
Estimated: FRE of ~$1.50B / management fees ~$2.42B = ~62%. FRE grew 19% to $1.50B. High margin reflects permanent capital fee stability and operating leverage.
FRE grew 19% in FY2025.

Computed: Market cap ~$20.9B (early Feb 2026) / FRE ~$1.50B = 14.0x. OWL trades at a significant discount to peers, partially due to high share count from SPAC structure and equity-based comp dilution.

data_points.missing_reason: OWL DE per-share ($0.84) implies total DE ~ $0.84 * ~1,550M diluted shares = ~$1.3B. P/DE = $20.9B / $1.3B = ~16.1x. However, OWL's complex multi-class share structure makes P/DE calculation uncertain.
data_points.extraction_notes: OWL has Class A, B, C, D shares plus earnout interests. Total diluted share count significantly exceeds basic shares. Best estimate P/DE ~ 16x.
Computed: Record $56B new capital commitments / beginning AUM $251B * 100 = 22.3%.

Q4 after-tax DE of $304M, $0.71/share. This is Q4 only; full-year DE/share not confirmed in search results. TPG uses 'after-tax distributable earnings'. Full-year estimate: ~$2.40/share based on quarterly run rate.
Q4 2024 after-tax DE of $261M, $0.62/share (Q4 only, highest quarterly since IPO). Full-year FY2024 DE not confirmed.
Fee-earning AUM (FAUM) of $170.1B as of December 31, 2025, up 20% YoY.
Total AUM of $303B as of December 31, 2025, up 23% YoY.
Total AUM of $246B at year-end 2024, up 11% YoY.
FAUM grew 20% YoY.
14% of AUM in perpetual funds, 10% of FAUM. Long-dated funds (10+ year duration) represent 67% of AUM and 69% of FAUM.
Credit platform AUM of $93B / total AUM $303B = 30.7%. Credit grew from $72B to $93B during 2025.
Record full-year FRE margin of 45%, expanding 340bps from FY2024. Q4 FRE margin reached 52%.
Full-year FY2024 FRE margin exceeded 40% (~41.6%, derived from 45% minus 340bps expansion).
Full-year FRE of $953M, up 25% YoY. Q4 FRE surged 72% YoY to $326M.

Computed: Management fees estimated ~$1.5B (Q4 $475M * 4 = $1.9B but growing; more conservatively from FRR $2.1B with ~71% management fees = ~$1.5B). Avg FEAUM [($141.8B + $170.1B)/2 = $155.95B]. Rate = $1.5B / $155.95B * 10,000 = 96.2 bps. Alternatively Q4 $475M * 4 / $155.95B = 121.8 bps (likely overestimate due to Q4 activation catch-ups).
Computed: Market cap ~$25.1B (Dec 2025, ~378M shares * ~$66.4) / FRE $953M = 26.3x.
Computed: Record $51.5B capital raised / beginning AUM $246B * 100 = 20.9%. 71% increase from $30.1B in FY2024.

partial_data_notes.BN: Classified as partial-alt. P/FRE not meaningful since market cap includes non-AM businesses (insurance, operating businesses). IFRS EPS not separately extracted. Asset management metrics shared with BAM.
partial_data_notes.APO: Uses ANI rather than traditional DE; commingled AM + insurance (Athene) balance sheet makes EV calculation less reliable. FGAUM includes Athene assets.

missing_data_reasons.GAAP_EPS_BAM_BN: BAM and BN report under IFRS, not US GAAP
missing_data_reasons.DE_APO_KKR: APO and KKR use Adjusted Net Income (ANI) rather than traditional DE; includes insurance segment earnings
missing_data_reasons.P_FRE_ARES: Full-year FRE dollar amount not precisely confirmed; estimated range 31-36x
missing_data_reasons.P_FRE_BN: Partial-alt; P/FRE not meaningful for holding company
missing_data_reasons.P_DE_OWL: Complex multi-class share structure makes total DE calculation uncertain

key_methodology_notes: Market caps are based on end-of-December 2025 data from MacroTrends and CompaniesMarketCap
Valuation multiples computed using market cap reference date of December 31, 2025
FRE margin definitions vary: BX pre-tax on fee-related revenue, CG on fee revenues, ARES on management and other fees
DE definitions vary significantly: BX after-tax with equity-comp deduction, ARES after-tax realized income, OWL distributable earnings, CG distributable earnings
APO and KKR include insurance businesses (Athene, Global Atlantic) complicating pure AM comparison
BAM and BN share the same underlying asset management platform; BAM is the asset-light manager, BN the holding company
Management fee rate computed as management fees / average FEAUM * 10,000 bps
Fundraising ratio = gross inflows / beginning-of-period AUM * 100
Permanent capital % = perpetual/evergreen AUM / total AUM * 100
